Background:
thumb|left|Sakura as an Academy student.
Sakura is the only child and daughter of Kizashi and Mebuki Haruno. She had a mostly normal childhood, raised by her mostly normal parents without any great tragedy or legendary family legacy, unlike her team members. When she entered Konoha's Academy, a few of the girls in her class started picking on her because of her broad forehead. Sakura tried to combat their teasing by hiding her forehead with her bangs, but this caused the girls to bully her even more. Ino Yamanaka, one of her classmates, saw this, and defended Sakura from her bullies and encouraged her to embrace her forehead rather than hide it.
Over the following years, Ino's guidance and friendship helped Sakura become comfortable with herself and develop into her own person. Though she felt indebted to Ino for helping her, Sakura began to feel that she was living in Ino's shadow when she, instead, wanted to be Ino's equal. At some point after she entered in the Academy, Sakura met Sasuke Uchiha and she developed a crush on him. When she told her friends, she was surprised to learn how popular Sasuke was with the girls. Sakura heard a rumour of Sasuke being attracted to girls with long hair and she began letting her hair grow to get his attention. A couple of years later, when she learned Ino had a crush on Sasuke and no longer wanting to be in Ino's shadow, Sakura ended their friendship so that they could compete for Sasuke's love, thus beginning a bitter rivalry between them and their dislike towards each other. After they were placed in their teams, Sakura approached Ino, bragging about being on Sasuke's team and declaring she won't lose.
Personality:
{{imgRight|Inner Sakura.png|Inner Sakura.}}
As a child, Sakura was insecure and highly self-conscious about her appearance, in particular her large forehead, covering it with her bangs. It was not until she met Ino, who defended her from bullies and became her very first friend, that she become more self-confident. At the start of Part I, Sakura typically gave the outward impression of being polite to authority figures, considerate of her peers, and confident in herself. She had occasional moments of competitiveness around her childhood friend Ino, but otherwise appeared quite collected. This reservation, though never truly disingenuous, frequently masked how Sakura felt: in certain situations, she had pronounced feelings of frustration, anger, envy, jealousy, mischief and joy. Rather than display these emotions to others too openly, she projected them inward, allowing a manifestation of who she truly is — what is labelled {{translation|"Inner Sakura"|内なるサクラ|Uchi Naru Sakura}} — to have the opinions Sakura wanted to keep to herself. Inner Sakura, whose appearances were marked by an exclamation of "Shannarō!", was in a sense a personality separate from Sakura herself, a fact that even allowed her to overcome the Mind Body Switch Technique. At other times, Sakura and Inner Sakura were indistinguishable from each other, usually with regards to her teammate Naruto Uzumaki. If he said or did something that annoyed or upset her, Sakura responded with violence, a reaction that under other circumstances would have been delegated to Inner Sakura.
thumb|left|Sakura's motivation to get stronger.
As Part I progresses, Sakura became increasingly exposed to the cruel realities of the shinobi world, difficulties for which she cannot rely upon Inner Sakura to cope with alone. When she was an Academy student, she succeeded through studying alone to the point of pride. As a shinobi, however, this is proven insufficient as missions cannot be truly completed merely with book smarts, and it is necessary to be able to fight so that other shinobi will not kill her or her teammates. The latter makes Sakura particularly disappointed in herself, as she was unable to contribute much in battle and had to rely on others to save her. She set out to change this about herself, dedicating nearly three years of training to making herself as capable as Naruto and Sasuke; in the meantime, she was willing to sacrifice herself to protect her friends and comrades.
Abilities:
thumb|left|Sakura defeating dozens of Ten-Tails clones.
As a genin, due to her focus on her studies, Sakura lacked any particular combat skill, other than the basic skills she learned in the Academy. This greatly limited her role in missions, to the point where she frequently noted how much she relied on her teammates, Sasuke and Naruto. On one occasion, she is even stated by Shikamaru to be: "A kunoichi with no particular talent". Determined to change herself, she undergoes intense training with Tsunade for two-and-a-half years. From Sakura's display of skill in the earlier portions of Part II, many notable ninja have remarked that she would likely surpass Tsunade. Upon completing the Strength of a Hundred Seal during the Fourth Shinobi World War, Sakura has finally caught up to her teammates at the time. Two years later, she rised to the rank of jōnin. By adulthood, her prowess has even led to her being recognized by Boruto as a suitable replacement for Naruto as Hokage.
[Chakra and Physical Prowess] While Sakura has rarely been seen using taijutsu in Part I, she was able to hold her own against Ino, one of the top students in her class, and her strikes likewise could launch people as seen from reprimanding Naruto. In Part II after Tsunade's training, Sakura's taijutsu improved tremendously. As taught by the Sannin, Sakura's taijutsu skill was mainly gained for the goal of evading harm on the battlefield to better ensure her duties as a medical-nin. To which, she showed remarkable reflexes and efficiency of movement. Against the Akatsuki member Sasori, both he and Chiyo were amazed at Sakura's ability to steadily read the flow of battle and dodge without taking damage. Her offensive skills are equally impressive, as by the time she becomes a jōnin, she is able to fend off dozens of  Toneri Ōtsutsuki's autonomous puppets with ease. Years later in the anime, her taijutsu as an adult further improves to where she can contend with a Mangekyō Sharingan wielding opponent like Shin Uchiha.thumb|Sakura releasing her Strength of a Hundred Seal.
As seen in Part I, Sakura's most defining skill was her proficiency in chakra control, which is considered the best within her generation.
